games.headline: Baltimore 13, Toronto 3
games.summary: Adley Rutschman powered the Orioles offense with four hits, including a home run and five RBI, leading Baltimore to a dominant 13-3 victory over the Blue Jays. Brandon Young pitched effectively to secure the win.

games.headline: Texas 3, Cleveland 2 — Gray (W)
games.summary: Corey Seager's two-run home run gave the Rangers the crucial runs they needed, as they narrowly defeated the Guardians 3-2. Parker Messick was tagged with the loss, while Jacob Latz secured the save in the close matchup.

games.headline: St. Louis 10, Cincinnati 3
games.summary: Hunter Dobbins pitched five dominant scoreless innings, leading the Cardinals to a decisive victory. Supported by a robust offense that included an A.Burleson home run, St. Louis routed the Reds 10-3.

games.headline: New York Blank San Diego, 5-0
games.summary: Christian Scott spearheaded a dominant pitching effort, silencing the Padres over 5.2 scoreless innings as the Mets shut out San Diego 5-0. J.Young and L.Torrens both homered, providing the offense needed to secure the Mets' shutout victory at Petco Park.

games.headline: Philadelphia 8, Chicago 6 — Bowlan (W)
games.summary: In an 8-6 slugfest where runs were abundant, the Phillies edged the White Sox. Kyle Schwarber led the Philadelphia offense with four hits, as Jonathan Bowlan secured the win and Jhoan Duran earned the save.

games.headline: Boston 5, New York 3 — Gray (W)
games.summary: W. Contreras delivered a key home run and three RBI, while A. Monasterio added another blast, driving the Red Sox to a 5-3 victory over the Yankees. S. Gray earned the win, with A. Chapman securing the save.

games.headline: Minnesota 5, Kansas City 3 — Matthews (W)
games.summary: B. Lee's two-run home run proved pivotal, providing the offensive spark the Twins needed in their 5-3 win over the Royals. Zebby Matthews pitched seven strong innings, allowing only two earned runs for the victory.

games.headline: Tampa Bay Blank Miami, 6-0
games.summary: Drew Rasmussen stifled the Marlins, striking out nine over seven scoreless innings. The Rays offense backed him, as R.Palacios and R.Vilade each drove in two runs, securing a dominant 6-0 victory.

games.headline: Los Angeles Blank Los Angeles, 1-0
games.summary: R. Sasaki fanned ten batters over seven dominant scoreless innings for the Angels, but Freddie Freeman's solo home run broke the deadlock. Blake Treinen closed out the tight 1-0 shutout, as the Dodgers edged past the Angels.

games.headline: Houston 5, Athletics 1 — Lambert (W)
games.summary: Isaac Paredes's four-RBI day, including a crucial home run, drove the Astros to a 5-1 victory over the Athletics. While M. Barnett pitched well for the Athletics, Peter Lambert secured the win after Jack Perkins gave up five earned runs.

games.headline: Detroit 7, Seattle 3 — Valdez (W)
games.summary: F.Valdez stifled the Mariners' offense with five innings of one-run ball, holding them at bay while the Tigers' bats capitalized on Bryan Woo. Detroit scored seven runs against Seattle pitching to secure a 7-3 victory at Comerica Park.

games.headline: Milwaukee 9, Colorado 7 (F/10)
games.summary: The deadlock finally broke in the tenth as J. Bauers drove in three runs, including the go-ahead score, propelling the Brewers to a 9-7 extra-innings win over the Rockies.

games.headline: San Francisco 18, Chicago 3
games.summary: The Giants' offense erupted with C. Schmitt hitting two home runs and W. Adames driving in four runs, while Robbie Ray pitched five scoreless innings. San Francisco routed the Cubs 18-3, handing Edward Cabrera the loss.

games.headline: Atlanta 6, Pittsburgh 3 — Pérez (W)
games.summary: Mitch Keller was tagged for six runs in just 4.2 innings, allowing the Braves to build an early lead against the Pirates. Martín Pérez picked up the victory as the Braves won 6-3.

games.headline: Washington 14, Arizona 1
games.summary: Washington's offense exploded for 14 runs in a decisive 14-1 blowout win against Arizona. L.Jr. led the charge with two home runs and six RBI, while Foster Griffin earned the win, allowing only one earned run over five innings.
